sections.paragraph:
fut. יִתַּךְ (kindred to נָסַךְ), to pour self out, to be poured out; always metaph., used of roaring, Job 3:24 of anger, 2Ch 12:7 34:25 Jer 42:18 44:6 of curses, Dan 9:11.

Niphal.-

(1) i.q. Kal to pour self out, used of water, rain, Exo 9:33 metaph. of anger, 2Ch 34:21 Jer 7:20.

(2) to flow down, to be melted, Eze 22:21 24:11 .

Hiphil הִתִּיךְ, once inf. הַנְתִּיךְ, Eze 22:20.-

(1) to pour out, to pour forth, Job 10:10 money, 2Ki 22:9.

(2) to melt, Eze 22:20.

Hophal, pass. ib. verse 22.

Derivative הִתּוּךְ.

[“נָתַל a root unused as a verb. Aram. ܢܬܰܓ i.q. Hebr. נָתַן to give. Hence תִּילֹון”].
